title: strychnos floribunda
intro: Strychnos floribunda, also known as the African poison nut, is a plant species native to tropical Africa. It is a small tree or shrub with toxic seeds that contain the alkaloids strychnine and brucine, which have been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The medicinal properties of Strychnos floribunda include its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic effects, making it beneficial for treating fever, rheumatism, and other ailments. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and decoctions, have been used to treat various health conditions, although caution is advised due to the toxic nature of its seeds.
